landing.sub: Whether you’re throwing it, working it, or hosting it — start here.

comingSoon.vndrBody: EvntCue’s Vndr portal opens to a curated first cohort. Verified Vndrs get matched to weddings and celebrations already on the platform — deposits flow through Stripe, and your calendar fills with events that fit.
comingSoon.venuSub: Open your calendar to qualified bookings
comingSoon.venuBody: EvntCue’s Venu portal opens this summer. Every request comes with date, guest count, and budget pre-qualified. Send back your packet, see deposits flow in through Stripe, and watch your calendar fill with the right kind of events.
comingSoon.success: On the list. We’ll be in touch the moment we open.

calculator.title: Budget Calculator
calculator.sub: Real DFW pricing. Drag whatever. Math keeps up.
calculator.subtypeSub: We’ll start your line items at DFW averages for that subtype, scaled to your guest count. You can adjust anything next.
calculator.megaHint: Past 500 covers, our team scopes events one-on-one — Venu holds, catering tiers, AV rigging.

warning.leadCalm: {label} events typically need {months}+ months of lead time — you’re comfortably inside that window.
warning.leadTightDanger: {label} events typically need {months}+ months of lead time — Venu holds, Vndr calendars, and permits fill up early. Expect a tighter shortlist and premium pricing for last-minute slots.
warning.leadTightWarn: {label} events typically need {months}+ months of lead time — Venu holds, Vndr calendars, and specialty bookings fill up early.
warning.coverLow: At {guests} guests, your event is much smaller than DFW typical for {label} (~{typical} guests). Cue can scope a more intimate Vndr list — some packages and Venu minimums won’t apply.
warning.coverHigh: At {guests} guests, your event runs larger than DFW typical for {label} (~{typical} guests). Larger Venus, expanded AV, and tier-up catering are likely needed.
warning.budgetLowTail: Vndr matches will skew budget-tier; some specialty options will be out of reach.
warning.budgetHighTail: Cue can scope premium and luxury-tier matches at this per-guest spend.
warning.proceedSupport: The rare combination — the long horizon and the room to do it well.

preview.yourDateSub: Picked from your {horizon} window — adjust anytime.
preview.yourDateFoot: This is what locks first — your Venu confirms against this date, then everything else scopes around it. You can change it later in the dashboard.
preview.cueCalmHeadline: Cue says — you’re in the normal planning window.
preview.cueTightHeadline: Cue says — this is very tight.
preview.cueBudgetFarBelow: Cue says — this budget is far below DFW typical.
preview.cueBudgetLuxury: Cue says — this budget is luxury-tier territory.
preview.cueCoverFar: Cue says — your cover count is well outside DFW typical.
preview.cueShortNotice: Cue says — this is short notice.
preview.cueBudgetLean: Cue says — your budget is on the lean side for DFW.
preview.cueBudgetAbove: Cue says — your budget runs above DFW typical.
preview.cueCoverAtypical: Cue says — your cover count is atypical for DFW.
preview.leadCalm: Most {plural} need {months}+ months of lead time — you’ve got room to breathe.
preview.leadTightDanger: Most {plural} need {months}+ months of lead time — Venu holds and permits fill up early.
preview.leadTightWarn: Most {plural} need {months}+ months of lead time — Venu holds and specialty bookings fill up early.
preview.coverLow: At {guests} guests, your event runs smaller than DFW typical ({typical} guests). Cue can scope a tighter, more intimate Vndr list.
preview.coverHigh: At {guests} guests, your event runs larger than DFW typical ({typical} guests). Larger Venus, expanded AV, and tier-up catering are likely needed.
preview.perGuestBelow: Per-guest at ${perGuest} sits below the DFW typical (~${typical}/guest) for a {noun} of this scope. Vndr matches will favor budget-tier; some specialty options will be out of reach.
preview.perGuestAbove: Per-guest at ${perGuest} sits above the DFW typical (~${typical}/guest) for a {noun} of this scope. Cue can scope premium and luxury-tier Vndr matches.
preview.perGuestCalm: Per-guest at ${perGuest} aligns with the DFW typical (~${typical}/guest) for a {noun} of this scope.
preview.cueProceedSupport: The rare combination — the long horizon and the room to do it well.
preview.cueCardBody: for your {noun}. Sign up and I’ll match Vndrs who fit your budget and style, draft your timeline, and send you five board-matched shortlists you can compare side by side.
preview.plnrSub: Cue’s read on your budget tier
preview.plnrBodyEm: Your event sits where a full-service Plnr earns their keep.
preview.plnrBody: They own your timeline end-to-end, fight for you on Vndr contracts and commission stacking, know the cultural traditions cold, and save you weeks of late-night coordination.
preview.vendorsSub: Venu first — everything else scopes around it
preview.vendorChipVndrHint: Florals, photo, music, AV — unlocks with Venu
preview.vendorFootnote: Catr and Vndr matches unlock once your Venu is booked. Every other Vndr scopes around the Venu’s catering rules, AV setup, and date — so locking the Venu first saves rework.
preview.ctaSub: Sign up free — your budget carries over, Vndr browsing unlocks, escrow is ready.
preview.ctaSubAuthed: You're signed in — drop this event onto your workspace and pick up planning.

login.signupSub: Your budget, milestones, and mood board are waiting. One step from here.
login.signupSubVndr: Your packages, inquiries, and bookings are waiting. One step from here.
login.signupSubVenu: Your spaces, inquiries, and calendar are waiting. One step from here.
login.signinSub: Sign in to pick up where you left off.
login.confirmHint: Open the link on this device so we can finish wiring your event into the workspace. The link expires in an hour.
login.footTerms: By continuing you agree to EvntCue’s terms.

recover.headline: Reset your password.
recover.sub: Enter your email and we'll send a link to set a new one.
recover.sentBody: If that email is on file, a reset link is on its way. Open it on this device to set a new password.
recover.sentHint: The link expires in an hour. If it doesn't land in a few minutes, check spam.

recoverSet.headline: Set a new password.
recoverSet.sub: Choose something you'll remember. Eight characters minimum.

moodBoard.title: Your board
moodBoard.emptyHint: Drag pieces around. Add a chip from the right, or bring in your own photos. Nothing leaves this board until you say so.
moodBoard.renderDisabledTooltip: Pin some chips and photos first.
moodBoard.slotLoading: Cue is bringing this slot to life…
moodBoard.spreadFooterCaption: An inspiration, not a guarantee. Built on EvntCue.
moodBoard.shareText: An inspiration from our mood board on EvntCue.
moodBoard.sharedPageCta: Want your own? EvntCue plans events end-to-end — Venus, Vndrs, the whole evening.
moodBoard.bringItInNote: Upload a JPG, PNG, WEBP, or HEIC up to 10 MB. Whatever you bring in is re-hosted privately to your board — never linked back out.
moodBoard.paletteStub: Chip palette lands in the next chunk.
moodBoard.urlPasteDisabled: Image-link paste lands in a future chunk.
moodBoard.urlImportSubtitle: We'll bring the pin to your board — no Pinterest signup needed.
moodBoard.urlImportSuccessCapped: Brought in {count} pins — your board hit its limit.
moodBoard.urlImportInvalid: That doesn't look like a Pinterest URL.
moodBoard.urlImportBoardTitle: That looks like a Pinterest board.
moodBoard.urlImportBoardBody: We'll bring in up to {remaining} pins from that board.
moodBoard.urlImportBoardBodyCap: You have {remaining} of 100 spots left — we'll bring in up to that many.
moodBoard.recentlyRemovedEmpty: Nothing here. Anything you remove shows up for 30 days, just in case.
moodBoard.recentlyRemovedWindow: Pins stay restorable for 30 days. After that they're gone.

dashboard.menuSettingsToast: Settings sheet lands in a future session.
dashboard.pickerEventNotFound: That event couldn't be found — showing your latest.
dashboard.cueAskToast: Cue is here. What do you need?
dashboard.tileGuestsUpgradeToast: Guest features unlock at $19.99/mo. Upgrade flow lands in Phase 4.

venuStart.title: Get listed on EvntCue
venuStart.headline: Let's get you
venuStart.sub: A few quick details and your dashboard is ready.
venuStart.nameLabel: Venu name · how guests know you

onboarding.metaDescription: List your work on EvntCue. Take real bookings. Your profile is free — we charge when you do.

hero.headlineAction: List your work. Take real bookings.
hero.sub: EvntCue is the operating layer where Orgnzs, Plnrs, and Venus coordinate one event in one shared system. Your profile is free — forever. You only pay when you book.

proof.takeHome: Of your booking, in your account

compare.h: Most platforms charge for <access>access.</access> We charge when you <paid>get paid.</paid>
compare.sub: All-in rate on a $5,000 booking. No listing fees, no subscription required to start.
compare.thumbtackNote: Pay-per-lead. Pay even if the lead never converts. Cheapest leads cost the most.
compare.eventbriteNote: Ticket-platform fees on top of payment processing.
compare.honeybookNote: Processing only. No marketplace, no leads, no escrow.
compare.evntcueNote: All-in on a $5,000 booking. No listing fee, no subscription, no surprise lines.

what.p: Free isn’t a teaser. It’s the listing, the calendar, the inbox, the payouts, and the AI that helps Plnrs find you. The Pro tier exists for Vndrs scaling past their solo workflow — not as a paywall around basic functionality.
what.aside: A free profile that earns is the only honest version of free. Everything else is a trial dressed up.

b1.name: Live profile, day one
b1.sub: Visible to every Plnr and Orgnz on the platform. Portfolio, packages, calendar, inbox — all unlocked at $0.

b2.name: Stripe Connect built in
b2.sub: Take deposits, balance payments, and tips through the platform. Funds release 48 hours after the event.

b3.name: Escrow that signals intent
b3.sub: Inquiries arrive with their qualification status visible. You know which leads are real before you respond.

b4.name: Cue does the matching
b4.sub: When a Plnr needs your category for an event you’re available for, Cue surfaces you. No pay-to-play.

calc.headline: For every <booking></booking> you book, you keep <keep></keep>.
calc.sub: We charge when you do — never before. Move the slider to your typical booking; the number on the right is what hits your account.
calc.deltaSavings: Pro saves you {amount} on this booking. Pro pays for itself at {frequency} at this size.
calc.deltaSameSize: At this size, free and Pro work out the same. Pro starts saving you money once your bookings cross $2,000.
calc.foot: No listing fee. No subscription required. No money out until money in. That’s the whole pricing model.

shell.sessionExpired: Your session expired. Sign in again to continue.
shell.saveFailed: Could not save right now. Try again.

s1.headline: First — what do you do?
s1.description: Pick your primary discipline. We’ll narrow it together. You can list multiple specialties below — right now we’re finding the heart of your work.
s1.cueText: Take your time. The category sets which events Cue surfaces you for, so it’s worth getting right — you can refine the sub-types right after.
s1.subtypeHead: Pick what fits · multiple OK · optional
s1.subtypeHelper: Your first pick is your primary specialty. Add more if your work genuinely spans them.
s1.subtypeCapHint: Most Vndrs pick one or two. Three or more is fine if your business really covers that ground.

s2.headline: Tell us about your business.
s2.description: This is what shows on your public profile. We’ll save it when you continue.
s2.businessNameHelp: This is what couples and Plnrs search for. Use the name on your invoices.
s2.emailHelp: Your login email. Change in settings later.
s2.phoneHelp: Day-of contact only. Never shown publicly until a booking confirms.
s2.serviceAreaHelp: Pick every neighborhood you cover. We’ll surface you to organizers in those areas. Multi-select.
s2.missingAreaAlert: We’re filling in DFW coverage as Vndrs join. Tell us where you cover by emailing team@evntcue.com — we’ll add it.
s2.foundingStoryLabel: In your own words — what do you do best?
s2.foundingStoryPlaceholder: Two or three sentences. What kind of work do you love? What’s the moment you keep coming back for?
s2.foundingStoryHelp: Cue uses this when matching you to events. Speak how you actually speak.
s2.sigDescription: Drop in 6–12 images of your own work. Cue reads the color, style, light, and composition — then matches you to organizers whose mood boards rhyme with yours.
s2.sigComingSoon: Coming next · open from your dashboard

s3.headline: Capacity, pricing, and how you like to work.
s3.description: Everything here is editable later. We’re finding a fair starting point — you’re always in control.
s3.cueText: Setting your starting price tells Plnrs what to expect at first contact. The system blocks double-booking past your set capacity.
s3.priceHelp: Your floor for an inquiry. Custom packages above this.
s3.priceBenchmark: A {price} starting price puts you in a comparable bracket to most DFW Vndrs at your scale.
s3.capacityHelp: How many events can you actively staff on the same day?
s3.capacityBenchmark: Most Vndrs list 1–2 concurrent events. The system blocks double-booking past your set capacity.
s3.pricingModelPackageDesc: Tiered packages — Good / Better / Best. Most Vndrs pick this.
s3.pricingModelHourlyDesc: Time-based billing with optional add-ons. Common for AV, content, mixology.
s3.bookingFlowInstantDesc: Confirmed clients can lock the date immediately when escrow funds.
s3.bookingFlowInquiryDesc: You review every lead before accepting. Recommended at launch.
s3.referralHelp: What you’ll offer Plnrs who refer events to you. Editable per package later.
s3.referralBenchmark: A {rate}% rate is visible to Plnrs on every search. You don’t pay it unless they actually book you through the platform.
s3.metaSavedOnContinue: Step 3 of 4 · fair starting points, never final

s4.headline: Documents and payouts — at your pace.
s4.description: Your profile goes live the moment you finish this step. Documents add a Verified badge that helps Plnrs pick you with confidence — nothing here blocks you from being listed.
s4.cueText: Most Vndrs come back to upload documents in their first week. The Verified badge makes a real difference once you’re competing for higher-end bookings — but it’s not a gate.
s4.escrowH: Escrow is the difference between a lead and a real booking.
s4.escrowSub: EvntCue holds Orgnz deposits in escrow until your contract is signed. You’ll see the qualification status on every inquiry we send you — so you know which leads are real before you respond.
s4.escrowStep1D: Deposit lands in EvntCue escrow. The inquiry arrives in your inbox tagged as Ready to book.
s4.escrowStep2D: Your terms, your contract. The escrow holds until both sides confirm.
s4.escrowStep3D: Funds release minus the platform fee and your tier’s booking commission.
s4.escrowFoot: It’s the difference between a job applicant who says “I’m interested” and one who says “I’ve already cleared my calendar for next Tuesday.” Both are interested. Only one is going to get the call back.
s4.stripeH: Connect Stripe for payouts · last step
s4.stripeD: Stripe Connect onboarding takes about 3 minutes. We do this last so you can finish the rest first — you’ll be redirected to Stripe and back to your dashboard automatically.
s4.metaFinal: Final step · your profile goes live next

editor.warningForm: Moving the event date can ripple to milestones, Vndr schedules, and timelines. We’ll show you what changes and what needs your approval before anything saves.
editor.warningPreviewEmpty: You’re changing your event date. Nothing else cascades — but this is your event date. Sure?
editor.warningPreviewItems: This change affects {count} item{plural}. Review before applying — nothing is saved yet.
editor.reasonPlaceholder: Why is this changing? Logged to event history.
editor.emptyCascadeNote: ✓ No milestones or custom items depend on this date yet — nothing else needs to shift.
editor.autoShiftDesc: These items are scheduled relative to your event date — they move with it automatically.
editor.needsApprovalDesc: These items have fixed dates — they stay put unless you check “shift it”.
editor.cueTextWarn: Most {category} bookings happen {recommended}+ months out. You’d be at {actual} months — some Vndrs may have limited availability.
editor.cueTextDanger: Most {category} bookings happen {recommended}+ months out. You’d be at {actual} months. Many Vndrs won’t be able to accommodate at this lead time.
editor.vendorTextSingle: 1 Vndr has a committed booking on this date. They'll be notified by email and in their portal Inquiries tab when you commit. You'll see their response in your feed.
editor.vendorTextMultiple: {count} Vndrs have committed bookings on this date. They'll be notified by email and in their portal Inquiries tab when you commit. You'll see their responses in your feed.

lock.headline: Lock it in?
lock.body: Once you lock, Vndr calendars block your slot and the confirmation goes out to anyone you’ve invited. You can still edit the date after locking — we’ll show you what cascades.

activation.headline: Set the date — make it real
activation.body: Your draft is your planning space. Set the date and your event becomes real — booking opens up so you can start lining up your team. You can change the date later; we’ll keep everyone posted.

commissionBreakdown.proCalloutVndrCatr: Pro removes listing caps and unlocks CRM, marketplace access, and integrated payments — most Vndrs book more on Pro than Free.
commissionBreakdown.proCalloutPlnrVenu: Pro drops your fee from 11% to 5% and unlocks marketplace access, commission automation, and CRM. Active operators upgrade for the savings + tools.
